description_long: This chair features a mid-century modern design with a gently curved plywood seat and backrest, both showcasing a natural wood grain pattern. The backrest is wide and slightly reclined, providing ergonomic support, while the seat is broad and smoothly contoured for comfort. The chair is supported by four angled wooden legs, each seamlessly integrated into the seat structure for stability. There are no armrests, giving the chair a minimalist and open appearance. The visible wood finish is smooth and matte, highlighting the natural texture and color variations of the material. This chair is reminiscent of classic Eames-style molded plywood seating, suitable for stylish urban interiors or public spaces.
description: A mid-century modern wooden chair with a curved plywood seat and backrest, supported by four angled legs and finished in a smooth, natural wood grain.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide, curved backrest and broad seat with visible wood grain, and the four angled legs.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the ergonomic curve of the backrest and seat, as well as the angled rear and front legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the full width of the backrest and the central support connecting it to the seat, with both rear legs visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the continuous curve of the seat and backrest, and the clean, armless profile.
